title: Keyboard and accessibility-tree audit deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because A complete keyboard, accessibility-tree, browser-zoom, and assistive-technology audit requires human and real-device evidence beyond one automated Chromium artifact.

items.title: Keyboard and accessibility-tree audit: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents misconception? A zero-error automated accessibility report certifies that the page is accessible.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Treat automation as bounded evidence and combine it with keyboard, structure, name/state, visual, and task-based human checks.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Treat automation as bounded evidence and combine it with keyboard, structure, name/state, visual, and task-based human checks.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents correction rather than the documented misconception.
